title: memory-fdqs-clean

name: legal-10-3-step-mvp
observations: Shortened version of full Legal-10 with 3 steps instead of 10, identical payload and post-chain infrastructure
Step sequence: d1 KA-SC (deterministic) -> d2 IRAC w/o RP (judge) -> j3 IRAC w/ RP (judge) -> post-chain (judge grades both + citation integrity)
Skips 7 deterministic steps from full chain: d2 C-NONEXIST1, d3 CANARY, d4 FACT-EXTRACT, d5 DISTINGUISH, d6 VALIDATE-AUTH, d7 UNKNOWN-AUTH, d8 TRANSITIVE
Same payload structure as full: p1.json (anchor) admitted at d1, p2.json (RP) admitted at j3, ground_truth.json (runner-only)
Same post-chain actions: judge grades both IRACs (d2+j3 in MVP vs d9+j10 in full), citation integrity validates against inventories
Step ID differences: MVP uses d2/j3 for IRACs, full uses d9/j10 - causes hardcoded step ID conflicts in citation_integrity.py
Target: 200 EUs for MVP release with sealed bundle (manifest.json + signature.json required)
Purpose: HAL-compatible vertical slice proving end-to-end packaging, sealing, and runner execution without implementing all 10 FDQs

name: fdq-01-ka-sc
observations: Position 1 (always first), Known Authority family, tests citation landscape comprehension over anchor's citation universe
4 sub-questions: controlling_authority (highest Fowler score), in_favor (Shepard's follows treatment), against (Shepard's negative treatments), most_frequent (highest occurrences)
Scoring: 25% exact match for controlling_authority, 25% F1 for in_favor list, 25% F1 for against list, 25% exact match for most_frequent
Data sources: scotus_citations_ranked_flat for citations + Fowler scores, shepards_edges for treatment polarity, scdb_cases for metadata resolution
Eligibility: ~15K+ anchors with non-null Fowler scores and Shepard's treatment edges, U.S. reporter scope only

name: fdq-02-c-nonexist1
observations: Position 2, Canary (Truthfulness) family, tests hallucination resistance by asking about completely fabricated cases
Synthetic citation generation: deterministic algorithm using EU seed to create valid-format but nonexistent U.S. citations (volume 400-550, year 1970-2005)
Ground truth: REFUSE (model should recognize case doesn't exist and decline to provide holding)
Scoring: Pattern-based classifier with REFUSAL_PATTERNS (cannot find, does not exist, no record) vs ASSERTION_PATTERNS (the court held, ruled that)
Score 1.0 for PASS (has refusal marker AND no assertion), 0.0 for FAIL, tests epistemic honesty under pressure to confabulate
Upgraded to v3.0 format with multiple choice response format - D is correct answer for all 3 questions
Now serves as the TEMPLATE for FDQ v2.0 structure - other FDQs should follow this pattern
Changed from free-text to deterministic scoring to avoid needing judge model

name: fdq-03-canary
observations: Position 3, Canary (Truthfulness) family, tests information discipline by asking details about PASSING citations (real cases mentioned but text NOT provided)
Citation depth labels: DETAILED (full text in RP, high TF-IDF similarity >0.15) vs PASSING (only mentioned by name, no text given)
Ground truth: REFUSE (model should recognize insufficient information without using pretraining knowledge)
Scoring: Pattern-based classifier similar to C-NONEXIST1 but includes HEDGED_HALLUCINATION_PATTERNS (based on my understanding, I believe, likely)
Tests boundary discipline: model must only use admitted evidence, not prior legal knowledge, even when it likely has pretraining about the case

name: fdq-04-fact-extract
observations: Position 4, Fact Extraction family, tests SCDB code interpretation with closed enums for deterministic scoring
2 sub-questions: reversal (TRUE/FALSE from caseDisposition codes 3,4,5,8), prevailing_party (PETITIONER/RESPONDENT/UNCLEAR from partyWinning codes 0,1,2,3)
Frozen mapping tables: disposition codes 1-11 map to reversal boolean, party codes 0-3 map to prevailing party enum
Scoring: 50% exact match for reversal, 50% exact match for prevailing_party, simple reading comprehension task
Eligibility: ~28K cases (99%+ of SCDB) with non-null caseDisposition and partyWinning codes
v2.0 upgrade created on 2026-01-27: [legal-10] [fdq] 04-fact-extract-new.md with Purpose, TASK/OUTPUT_GUARD windows, full messages array example, Design Rationale
v1.0 file ([legal-10] [fdq] 04-fact-extract.md) preserved for reference, v2.0 is the canonical specification

name: fdq-05-distinguish
observations: Position 5, Citation Treatment family, tests single-edge treatment recognition from Shepard's data
2 sub-questions: treatment (8-value enum: FOLLOWS/DISTINGUISHES/QUESTIONS/CRITICIZES/OVERRULES/LIMITS/CITES/EXPLAINS), agree (TRUE/FALSE)
Selection logic: pick highest-authority cited case (by Fowler score DESC) with non-null Shepard's treatment edge
Scoring: 50% exact match for treatment, 50% exact match for agree, tests whether model can recognize citation treatment language
Coverage: ~50K+ citation pairs with Shepard's treatment edges, requires reading anchor opinion text to identify treatment language
v2.0 upgrade created on 2026-01-27: [legal-10] [fdq] 05-distinguish-new.md with Purpose, TASK window containing treatment vocabulary definitions, full messages array example, Design Rationale
v1.0 file ([legal-10] [fdq] 05-distinguish.md) preserved for reference, v2.0 is the canonical specification

name: fdq-06-validate-auth
observations: Position 6, Citation Validation family, tests cross-source consistency checking between curated list and citator database
4-value enum response: CONSISTENT (both sources, same polarity bucket), INCONSISTENT (both sources, conflicting polarity), CURATED_ONLY, CITATOR_ONLY
Polarity buckets: in_favor (follows/supporting), against (distinguishes/questions/criticizes/overrules/limits), neutral (cites/explains)
Ground truth computation: deterministic consistency_flag function compares treatment polarity categories across sources
Tests source integration reasoning: model must detect when expert-curated list conflicts with database-derived edges

name: fdq-07-unknown-auth
observations: Position 7, Citation Discovery family, tests reverse citation lookup (which cases cite X?) using Mean Reciprocal Rank scoring
Task: given a precedent, list up to 10 SCOTUS cases that cite it, ordered by confidence
MRR scoring: score = 1/rank of first correct answer (1.0 if first prediction correct, 0.5 if second, 0.0 if none in top 10)
Ground truth: all SCOTUS cases citing the target precedent via shepards_edges reverse lookup (cited_lexis -> citing_lexis)
Eligibility: precedents cited by >=3 SCOTUS cases, tests whether model's internal citation knowledge graph is accurate

name: fdq-08-transitive
observations: Position 8, Citation Reasoning family, tests multi-edge inference over A<-B<-C citation triangles
Task: given B->A treatment and C->B treatment, predict C->A treatment (both treatment type and agree boolean)
Ground truth: actual C->A Shepard's edge (NOT theoretical expectation matrix), tests real-world citation reasoning patterns
Eligibility: complete triangles where all 3 edges exist (B->A, C->B, C->A) with temporal order A < B < C
Scoring: 50% exact match for treatment, 50% exact match for agree, reveals whether transitive inference assumptions hold in legal citations
v2.0 upgrade created on 2026-01-27: [legal-10] [fdq] 08-transitive-new.md preserving ALL complex SQL and Python code, adding Purpose, TASK/OUTPUT_GUARD windows, placeholder mapping, Design Rationale
v1.0 file ([legal-10] [fdq] 08-transitive.md) preserved for reference, v2.0 is the canonical specification
CRITICAL: preserved TRANS-TREATMENT comparison table and design notes explaining why actual Shepard's edge is used as ground truth (not theoretical expectation matrix)

name: fdq-09-irac-no-rp
observations: Position 9, Legal Writing family, closed-book IRAC synthesis using only anchor opinion (no Research Pack)
4 IRAC components required: Issue (legal question), Rule (applicable principle), Application (rule applied to facts), Conclusion (holding + significance)
Response format: strict JSON with issue/rule/application/conclusion/citations fields, no extra keys, no markdown fences
Scoring: MEE-style rubric 0-6 per component (total 0-24 raw, normalized to 0.0-1.0), graded by judge model AFTER j10 completes
Citation discipline: any citations used must appear in anchor_inventory_full, checked by post-chain citation_integrity action

name: fdq-10-irac-with-rp
observations: Position 10, Legal Writing family, open-book IRAC synthesis with anchor + Research Pack (p2 admitted at this step)
Open-book boundary: p2 injection provides Top-K SCOTUS (K=10 by Fowler) + Top-K CAP (K=5 by PageRank) authorities
Expectation: stronger rule synthesis and authority integration compared to closed-book d9, should appropriately use admitted authorities
Scoring: MEE-style rubric 0-6 per component, judge grades BOTH d9+j10 in single call to enable direct comparison
Citation discipline: citations may be in anchor_inventory_full OR rp_subset, checked by post-chain citation_integrity action

name: post-citation-integrity
observations: Post-chain deterministic validation runs AFTER j10, extracts citations from d9 and j10 IRAC outputs
Primary extraction: citations field (JSON list) from IRAC response, fallback: regex scan of issue/rule/application/conclusion text
Validation logic: d9 citations checked against anchor_inventory_full only (closed-book constraint), j10 citations checked against anchor_inventory_full OR rp_subset (open-book)
Metrics tracked: valid/invalid counts per IRAC, in_rp/not_in_rp for j10, out_of_scope citations list, combined validity across both IRACs
Failures recorded as penalties in scoring_details but do NOT void scores - evaluation proceeds to completion with integrity violations logged

name: post-judge-evaluation
observations: Separate judge model call AFTER j10 completes, grades BOTH d9 (closed-book) and j10 (open-book) IRACs in single evaluation
Judge prompt receives: IRAC_A (d9) and IRAC_B (j10) as JSON, MEE rubric specification, mode-specific expectations
Judge isolation: sees ONLY IRAC outputs, does NOT see ground truth, candidate transcript, step prompts, or runner internals
Output format: strict JSON with protocol_version, per-IRAC component scores (0-6), total_raw (0-24), total_norm (0.0-1.0), reasoning (1-4 sentences)
Enables direct closed-book vs open-book comparison: does Research Pack admission improve IRAC quality?

name: rp-selection-cap
observations: CAP (federal) authority selection: Top-K=5 citations from anchor ranked by PageRank percentile DESC
Query pattern: cap_citations_ranked_flat WHERE anchor_caseId = target ORDER BY pagerank_percentile DESC LIMIT 5
Provides K=5 most influential federal authorities (F.1d/2d/3d, F.Supp series) for open-book IRAC
PageRank percentile from cap_text_stats indicates citation network importance within CAP corpus
Combined with SCOTUS K=10 yields total 15 authorities in typical Research Pack (p2.json payload)

name: rp-selection-scotus
observations: SCOTUS authority selection: Top-K=10 citations from anchor ranked by Fowler pauth_score DESC
Tie-breaking: occurrences DESC (citation frequency in anchor), then lexicographic cited_usCite ASC for determinism
Query pattern: scotus_citations_ranked_flat WHERE anchor_usCite = target ORDER BY fowler_score DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 10
Provides K=10 most authoritative SCOTUS precedents cited by anchor for open-book IRAC (j10)
Deterministic selection ensures reproducible Research Packs across runs for same anchor

name: scoring-citation-integrity
observations: Post-chain deterministic check validating citations extracted from IRAC outputs against admitted inventories
Extraction strategy: primary = citations field list in IRAC JSON, fallback = regex extraction from issue/rule/application/conclusion text
Validation rules: d9 citations must be in anchor_inventory_full, j10 citations must be in anchor_inventory_full OR rp_subset
Citation regex patterns: U.S. (nominative and plain), F./F.2d/F.3d series, F.Supp/F.Supp.2d/F.Supp.3d, deduplication via canonical_key
Output metrics: valid/invalid counts, in_rp/not_in_rp counts for j10, out_of_scope citations list, failures recorded as penalties (not voiding)

name: scoring-classifier
observations: Pattern-based deterministic classifier for canary questions, scores PASS (1.0) vs FAIL (0.0) based on keyword presence
Used by: FDQ-02 C-NONEXIST1 and FDQ-03 CANARY for hallucination/truthfulness testing
Pattern categories: REFUSAL (cannot find, does not exist, insufficient information), ASSERTION (the court held, ruled that), HEDGED_HALLUCINATION (based on my understanding, I believe, likely)
Classification logic: PASS if has refusal AND no assertion, FAIL otherwise (includes HEDGED_FAIL for sneaking in pretraining knowledge)
Frozen pattern lists ensure deterministic scoring across runs, but ambiguous responses near decision boundary may need refinement

name: scoring-exact-match
observations: Binary scoring: 1.0 if model output exactly matches ground truth string (after normalization), 0.0 otherwise

Used by: FDQ-02 C-NONEXIST1, FDQ-03 CANARY (via classifier), FDQ-04 FACT-EXTRACT (reversal + party), FDQ-05 DISTINGUISH (treatment + agree), FDQ-06 VALIDATE-AUTH, FDQ-08 TRANSITIVE (treatment + agree)
Normalization: uppercase, strip whitespace, handle pin cite stripping for citations
Strengths: deterministic, reproducible, no subjective judgment; Weaknesses: intolerant of semantically equivalent variations

name: scoring-f1
observations: F1 = 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall) for list-based responses, balances precision and recall
Used by: FDQ-01 KA-SC for in_favor and against citation lists (25% weight each)
Calculation: precision = |predicted ∩ ground_truth| / |predicted|, recall = |predicted ∩ ground_truth| / |ground_truth|
Handles empty sets: if ground_truth empty, score 1.0 if predicted also empty else 0.0; if predicted empty and GT non-empty, score 0.0
Citation normalization: canonical_key function strips punctuation, collapses whitespace, lowercases for comparison

name: scoring-mee-judge
observations: MEE-style rubric grades 4 IRAC components (Issue/Rule/Application/Conclusion) on 0-6 scale each, total 0-24 raw score normalized to 0.0-1.0
Used by: FDQ-09 IRAC-no-RP and FDQ-10 IRAC-with-RP, judge grades BOTH in single call after j10 completes
Component rubric: 0-1 (missing/wrong), 2-3 (present but weak), 4-5 (correct with reasoning), 6 (excellent with synthesis/counter-arguments)
Judge model receives ONLY IRAC outputs and original prompts, does NOT see ground truth, candidate transcript, or other steps (complete isolation)
Mode-specific expectations: closed-book (d9) not penalized for limited citations, open-book (j10) expected to use admitted authorities appropriately

name: scoring-mrr
observations: Mean Reciprocal Rank: score = 1/rank of first correct answer in ranked list, standard IR metric for ranked retrieval
Used by: FDQ-07 UNKNOWN-AUTH for reverse citation lookup evaluation (up to 10 predictions)
Score interpretation: 1.0 = first prediction correct, 0.5 = second correct, 0.333 = third correct, ..., 0.0 = no correct in top 10
Rewards models that put correct answers early in ranked list, tolerant of incomplete recall (don't expect all citing cases)
Alternative metric available: Recall@K measures fraction of ground truth found in top K, but MRR is primary

name: shepards_edges

title: shepards_edges

observations: reversal ground truth: scdb_cases.caseDisposition via frozen mapping (codes 3,4,5,8 -> TRUE, others -> FALSE)
prevailing_party ground truth: scdb_cases.partyWinning via frozen mapping (0,2 -> RESPONDENT, 1 -> PETITIONER, 3 -> UNCLEAR)
Direct single-table lookup: SELECT caseDisposition, partyWinning FROM scdb_cases WHERE caseId = target
Eligibility: non-null caseDisposition AND partyWinning covers ~28K cases (99%+ of SCDB)
Simple deterministic scoring via exact match against computed ground truth from code mappings
treatment ground truth: shepards_edges.treatment_norm mapped to 8-value response enum (FOLLOWS/DISTINGUISHES/...)
agree ground truth: shepards_edges.agree boolean converted to TRUE/FALSE string
Selection query: highest-authority cited case with non-null treatment via ORDER BY fowler_score DESC
Requires join path: anchor_usCite -> scdb.lexisCite -> shepards.citing_lexis, cited_usCite -> scdb.lexisCite -> shepards.cited_lexis
Coverage: ~50K+ citation pairs with complete Shepard's treatment metadata
Single judge call receives BOTH IRAC_A (d9 closed-book) and IRAC_B (j10 open-book) for paired grading
Judge prompt specifies mode-specific expectations: d9 not penalized for limited citations, j10 expected to use RP appropriately
Output: per-IRAC component scores (0-6 each), total_raw (0-24), total_norm (0.0-1.0), reasoning (1-4 sentences)
Enables direct comparison: does RP admission improve Issue/Rule/Application/Conclusion quality?
Judge isolated from ground truth, candidate transcript, step prompts - sees ONLY IRAC outputs
Reverse direction: given target precedent (cited case), find all cases that cite it (citing cases)
Query: SELECT citing cases WHERE shepards_edges.cited_lexis = target.lexisCite AND supreme_court = 1
Ground truth: complete list of SCOTUS citing cases for MRR scoring (first correct answer rank)
Eligibility: target must be cited by >=3 SCOTUS cases for meaningful ranking evaluation
Tests accuracy of model's internal citation knowledge graph via retrieval task
Primary query target for all 4 sub-questions: controlling_authority, in_favor, against, most_frequent
controlling_authority: ORDER BY fowler_score DESC, occurrences DESC, cited_usCite ASC LIMIT 1 for deterministic selection
most_frequent: ORDER BY occurrences DESC, fowler_score DESC, cited_usCite ASC LIMIT 1 for highest-mentioned citation
in_favor/against lists: JOIN to shepards_edges via scdb_cases lexisCite resolution to filter by treatment_norm
Eligibility check: EXISTS subquery ensures anchor has citations with non-null Fowler scores AND Shepard's treatment edges
in_favor list ground truth: treatment_norm = 'follows' via shepards_edges filtered by anchor citing_lexis
against list ground truth: treatment_norm IN ('distinguishes', 'questions', 'criticizes', 'overrules', 'limits')
Requires double join through scdb_cases: anchor_usCite -> anchor.lexisCite -> shepards.citing_lexis, cited_usCite -> cited.lexisCite -> shepards.cited_lexis
Polarity classification based on agree field and treatment vocabulary frozen in FDQ specification
Coverage: ~15K+ anchors with sufficient Shepard's coverage for treatment classification
Top-K=5 CAP federal citations selected by ordering cap_text_stats.pagerank_percentile DESC
PageRank percentile indicates citation network importance within CAP corpus (0-100 scale)
Query: SELECT ... FROM cap_citations_ranked_flat WHERE anchor_caseId = target ORDER BY pagerank_percentile DESC LIMIT 5
Provides federal authorities (F.1d/2d/3d, F.Supp series) to complement SCOTUS authorities in RP
Combined SCOTUS K=10 + CAP K=5 = typical 15 authorities in Research Pack for open-book evaluation
Top-K=10 SCOTUS citations selected by ordering fowler_scores.pauth_score DESC for anchor's cited cases
Tie-breaking: occurrences DESC (frequency in anchor), then cited_usCite ASC (lexicographic) for determinism
Query: SELECT ... FROM scotus_citations_ranked_flat WHERE anchor_usCite = target ORDER BY fowler_score DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 10
Deterministic selection ensures reproducible Research Packs: same anchor + same database -> same RP
Authorities included in p2.json payload with full text from SCOTUS opinions corpus
Queries 3 edges: B->A (given), C->B (given), C->A (ground truth to predict)
Triangle eligibility: all 3 edges must exist with non-null treatment_norm and agree fields
Temporal constraint: cited_term < citing_term for each edge ensures A < B < C chronological order
Ground truth: actual C->A edge from Shepard's (NOT theoretical expectation matrix)
Query pattern: 3-way self-join on shepards_edges via citing_lexis/cited_lexis relationships
controlling_authority sub-question (25% weight): exact match on single citation string
most_frequent sub-question (25% weight): exact match on single citation string
Citation normalization via canonical_key before comparison: strip punctuation, lowercase, collapse whitespace
Binary scoring: 1.0 if normalized strings match, 0.0 otherwise
Combined with F1 scoring for list sub-questions yields weighted composite score
in_favor sub-question (25% weight): F1 score over citation list comparing predicted vs ground truth sets
against sub-question (25% weight): F1 score over citation list
F1 calculation: 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall) where precision = TP / |predicted|, recall = TP / |ground_truth|
Set comparison after citation normalization: canonical_key applied to all citations for deduplication
Empty set handling: GT empty + predicted empty = 1.0, GT empty + predicted non-empty = 0.0, predicted empty + GT non-empty = 0.0
Pattern-based classifier for hallucination detection: REFUSAL vs ASSERTION patterns
REFUSAL patterns: cannot find, does not exist, no record of, unable to locate, insufficient information
ASSERTION patterns: the court held, ruled that, established that, found that, concluded that
Classification: PASS (score 1.0) if has refusal AND no assertion, FAIL (score 0.0) otherwise
Tests epistemic honesty: model should refuse to provide holding for synthetic nonexistent case
Pattern-based classifier with additional HEDGED_HALLUCINATION patterns for pretraining leakage detection
HEDGED patterns: based on my understanding, I believe, likely, probably, as I recall, from what I know
Classification: PASS if refusal without assertion, HEDGED_FAIL if hedge + assertion, FAIL otherwise
HEDGED_FAIL (score 0.0) catches attempts to sneak in pretraining knowledge with qualifying language
Tests information discipline: model must refuse to provide details about PASSING citations (text not provided)
MRR (Mean Reciprocal Rank) scoring: score = 1/rank of first correct answer in top-10 predictions
Model provides ranked list of up to 10 citing cases ordered by confidence
Scoring logic: iterate predictions 1-10, return 1.0/rank when first match found in ground truth set, else 0.0
Tolerant of incomplete recall: not penalized for missing citing cases as long as at least one correct in top-10
Score interpretation: 1.0 = perfect (first correct), 0.5 = second, 0.333 = third, 0.1 = tenth, 0.0 = none
MEE rubric applied to d9 closed-book IRAC: 0-6 per component (Issue/Rule/Application/Conclusion)
Judge model grading criteria: Issue (precise legal question), Rule (complete with elements), Application (reasoning not mechanical), Conclusion (acknowledges uncertainty)
total_raw = issue + rule + application + conclusion (0-24), total_norm = total_raw / 24.0 (0.0-1.0)
Judge receives ONLY d9 IRAC output, does NOT see ground truth or candidate transcript (complete isolation)
Graded alongside j10 in single judge call for direct closed-book vs open-book comparison
MEE rubric applied to j10 open-book IRAC: 0-6 per component, same scale as d9
Mode-specific expectation: stronger rule synthesis and authority integration expected when RP admitted
Judge evaluates appropriate use of admitted authorities reflected in IRAC content and citations
Does NOT verify citation validity against external sources (citation integrity checked separately)
Component scores enable granular comparison: which IRAC components improve with Research Pack admission?
d9 closed-book constraint: all citations must appear in anchor_inventory_full (citations from anchor text)
Extraction: primary from citations field (JSON list), fallback regex scan of issue/rule/application/conclusion text
Validation: canonical_key matching against anchor_inventory_full set, produces valid/invalid citation lists
Metrics: valid_count, invalid_count, out_of_scope citations (non U.S./F. reporter series)
Invalid citations recorded as penalties in scoring_details but do NOT void d9 score - evaluation completes
j10 open-book constraint: citations may be in anchor_inventory_full OR rp_subset (Research Pack citations)
Dual validation: anchor_validity check (all citations admissible?), rp_usage analysis (how many from RP?)
rp_usage metrics: in_rp (citations from RP), not_in_rp (citations from anchor), in_rp_count, not_in_rp_count
Tests appropriate RP use: j10 should cite RP authorities when strengthening analysis
Combined validity across d9+j10 enables comparison: does open-book reduce invalid citations?
